name: PubNub API
what_it_does: Provides a managed real-time publish/subscribe messaging network with presence detection, message persistence, and WebSocket delivery, accessible via REST API and SDKs using publish/subscribe key pair authentication.
use_cases: Publish real-time status updates or alerts from agent workflows to subscribed client applications without polling
Implement presence detection to track which users or agent instances are currently active in a channel
Deliver live data feeds (scores, prices, sensor readings) from agent-processed sources to browser or mobile clients
Coordinate distributed agent instances by using PubNub channels as a lightweight inter-agent message bus
Build real-time collaborative features where agent-generated content is broadcast instantly to multiple recipients
not_for: Storing or querying large historical datasets — PubNub message persistence is limited in duration and not a substitute for a time-series database
Guaranteed exactly-once message delivery in critical financial or transactional workflows where at-most-once pub/sub semantics are insufficient
Heavy server-to-server REST API usage patterns where a message queue like SQS or RabbitMQ would be more appropriate
best_when: Best when an agent needs to push real-time updates to browser or mobile clients at scale using a managed pub/sub network without operating WebSocket infrastructure.
avoid_when: Avoid when the use case requires durable message queuing with acknowledgment semantics, as PubNub is optimized for live broadcast rather than reliable task queue delivery.

auth.notes: Uses a publish key and subscribe key pair. Access Manager add-on provides token-based authorization with channel-level read/write/manage scopes. Basic usage requires only the key pair; Access Manager is an optional paid add-on.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier includes 1 million transactions/month and 1 GB data transfer; a transaction is counted per message delivered to a connected client
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter ~$49/month; Pro plans scale with transaction volume; Enterprise pricing custom. Message persistence and Access Manager are paid add-ons.
pricing.notes: The transaction model can make costs hard to predict at scale since each message recipient counts as a transaction. Message history storage is billed separately.

performance.rate_limits: Publish rate limits depend on plan; default allows thousands of messages per second; subscribe connections scale to millions concurrently

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors are returned as JSON with status codes and a description field. SDK-level errors are well documented but vary in consistency across language implementations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Publish operations are fire-and-forget with no idempotency keys. Duplicate message handling must be implemented by consumers using message timestamps or custom IDs.
known_agent_gotchas: Transaction-based pricing means a message broadcast to 1,000 subscribers costs 1,000 transactions — cost can explode unexpectedly in fan-out scenarios
The publish and subscribe keys are different — using the wrong key for an operation produces confusing authentication errors
Message size is limited to 32KB by default; agents sending large payloads must chunk or compress data before publishing
Access Manager tokens must be refreshed before expiry; agents using fine-grained channel permissions need a token refresh workflow
WebSocket connections from server-side agents count against concurrent connection limits which vary by plan and are not always clearly documented

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified. HIPAA BAA available. Access Manager provides channel-level authorization with signed tokens. Publish key should never be exposed in client-side applications without Access Manager restricting permissions.
